questions.question: Why are intermediate CAs commonly used?
options: To protect root CA keys by keeping them offline
To remove the need for certificates
questions.description: Intermediates sign operational certificates while root keys can remain highly protected.

questions.question: Which certificate extension constrains valid DNS names?
questions.description: SAN entries define the DNS names or IP addresses a certificate is valid for.

questions.question: Which failure should a client report when the certificate chain cannot reach a trusted root?
questions.description: A chain must terminate at a trusted root CA to be accepted.
